author: phase-4-coordinator
content:
Resolved via option 2 from the issue discussion: added Array-aware forward-mode AD primitives that accept ferray Arrays directly. Implementing Element for DualNumber<T> was the other option but would require breaking the sealed trait in ferray-core (the two crates are in a downstream relationship that can't become a cycle).

New module ferray-autodiff/src/array_ops.rs exposes five functions:

  derivative_elementwise(f, &Array<T, D>) -> Array<T, D>
    Elementwise f'(x) for scalar→scalar f, preserving input shape.

  value_and_derivative_elementwise(f, &Array<T, D>) -> (values, derivs)
    Both in one pass.

  gradient_vector(f, &Array<T, Ix1>) -> Array<T, Ix1>
    Full gradient of a scalar-valued f: R^n → R via n forward passes.

  value_and_gradient(f, &Array<T, Ix1>) -> (T, Array<T, Ix1>)

  jacobian_array(f, &Array<T, Ix1>) -> Array<T, Ix2>
    m×n Jacobian of a vector-valued f: R^n → R^m. Renamed to avoid
    colliding with the existing slice-based jacobian() already in the
    crate.

Generic over T: Element + Float so f32 and f64 both work. Added
ferray-core as a direct dep. 12 new unit tests cover: elementwise x^2,
sin, x^3 on a 2-D array, value_and_derivative, quadratic-form
gradient, value_and_gradient, linear-map Jacobian, polar-to-cartesian
Jacobian, inconsistent-output-dim rejection, and f32 coverage. Plus
3 new doctests on the public API.

112 lib tests (up from 100) + 15 property + 9 doctests pass. Clippy
clean. Full workspace builds.
